title: DiceRoller - physics engine 3D dice rolling simulation
description: DiceRoller is a cross-platform app I built to deliver a fun and interactive 3D dice-rolling experience across both mobile and web.
descriptionContinued: On mobile, users can simply shake their phone to roll the dice, while on the web, they can use the WASD keys for keyboard input to move the dice. I built the app using Expo and React Native, and integrated expo-three for real-time 3D rendering and Cannon.js for realistic physics.
threeDDiceSimulationDesc: Rendered realistic 3D dice using expo-three and applied a physics engine with Cannon.js.
crossPlatformSupportDesc: Built with React Native and Expo, supporting both mobile and web platforms.
shakeDetectionMobileDesc: Used react-native-shake to detect device motion and roll the dice by shaking the phone.
keyboardInteractionWebDesc: Enabled WASD key input to roll the dice in the browser environment.
cameraPositionLightingDesc: Implemented a fixed camera angle and ambient lighting to enhance visibility and realism.
appWalkthroughDescription: Watch how users can roll the dice by physically shaking their phone in this mobile walkthrough.
videoNotSupported: Your browser does not support the video tag.
